title: Reproducible chest pain after lifting

chart.oneLiner: 26M presents with 3 days of sharp left-sided chest pain that began after an intense weightlifting session, worse with movement and deep breaths.

opening: I've got this sharp pain on the left side of my chest. It started after I was lifting at the gym a few days ago.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 26-year-old man, healthy-appearing, slightly worried he's having heart trouble. Cooperative.

HPI:
- Sharp, well-LOCALIZED pain over the left anterior chest for 3 days.
- Began after a heavy chest workout (bench press) at the gym (only mentions the gym if asked what he was doing when it started).
- Worse with MOVEMENT, twisting, reaching, and DEEP BREATHS; better with rest and staying still.
- He can point to the exact spot with one finger and pressing on it reproduces the pain.
- NO exertional pattern with walking, NO pressure/heaviness, NO diaphoresis, NO radiation to arm/jaw, NO dyspnea at rest, NO fever, NO cough, NO recent travel or leg swelling.

OLDCARTS: onset 3 days ago after exercise; left anterior chest, pinpoint; intermittent; sharp; worse with movement and palpation and deep breath; severity 4/10.

PMH: none. MEDICATIONS: none. ALLERGIES: none. FAMILY: noncontributory. SOCIAL: nonsmoker, occasional alcohol, lifts weights regularly, no drugs.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY the system/maneuver requested; give normals when asked):
- General: well-appearing, comfortable at rest.
- Vitals: BP 122/74, HR 72, RR 14, 36.8°C, SpO2 100% RA.
- Chest wall: REPRODUCIBLE point tenderness over the left 4th–5th costochondral junctions; palpation recreates his pain.
- Cardiac: regular, normal S1/S2, no murmur or rub.
- Lungs: clear bilaterally, good air entry.
- Extremities: no edema, no calf tenderness, symmetric pulses. Abdomen: soft, nontender.

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis.
- Answer only what is asked.
- Do NOT volunteer the gym/weightlifting trigger unless asked what brought it on or what he was doing.
- The reproducible tenderness is only revealed if the student palpates the chest wall.
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your history and physical, list your differential diagnosis (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Musculoskeletal chest pain / costochondritis (leading, 9; accepts chest wall strain). Considers and reasonably deprioritizes ACS given age, no risk factors, reproducible pain (CANT-MISS to at least consider,4). PE (bonus,2). Pericarditis (bonus,2). Pneumothorax (bonus,2). Names costochondritis with supporting features — reproducible tenderness, exercise trigger, low pretest probability (core,5). PENALTY: missing that reproducible tenderness does not fully exclude cardiac causes in higher-risk patients (note only; no penalty here given very low pretest probability).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: Costochondritis. Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: Considers and reasonably deprioritizes ACS given age, no risk factors, reproducible pain. Also reasonable: .

criticalActions.item: Considers and reasonably deprioritizes ACS given age, no risk factors, reproducible pain

coreActions.item: Names costochondritis with supporting features — reproducible tenderness, exercise trigger, low pretest probability

mustNotMiss: Considers and reasonably deprioritizes ACS given age, no risk factors, reproducible pain

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What workup, if any, do you want to order? (Consider pretest probability and avoiding over-testing.)
steps.rubric: Recognizes this is a clinical diagnosis with very low cardiac pretest probability (8). A single screening EKG is reasonable for reassurance (5). Appropriately AVOIDS an extensive/serial cardiac workup, stress testing, or CT angiogram in a low-risk young patient (8; reward restraint). Documents red flags that would prompt escalation (4). PENALTY: ordering CT angiogram / stress test / serial troponins with no indication (-4 for over-testing).

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = normal sinus rhythm, no ischemic changes. CREDIT: rate & rhythm (7); recognizes a normal tracing (5); systematic read (3). PENALTY: over-reading a normal EKG (-2).
keyFindings: labels them nonspecific and recommends comparison/serials
commonMistakes: over-calling nonspecific T inversions as a definite acute coronary syndrome
steps.idealAnswer: Nonspecific T-wave inversions in the lateral leads; recommend comparison with prior and serial tracings.

coreActions: labels them nonspecific and recommends comparison/serials

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = no acute cardiopulmonary process. CREDIT: identifies a normal film (6); notes it excludes other causes (4). PENALTY: inventing pathology (-2).
commonMistakes: dismissing the nodule as a nipple shadow without confirmation
missing it on a busy film
steps.idealAnswer: Solitary pulmonary nodule (~1.5 cm); recommend comparison with prior imaging and structured follow-up.

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: What is your leading diagnosis and your reasoning?
steps.rubric: Costochondritis / musculoskeletal chest pain (9). Justifies: reproducible point tenderness, mechanical trigger, pleuritic-with-movement quality, young low-risk patient (4). Notes the limits — would reconsider if features evolve (2).

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH chest pain algorithm (MSK/costochondritis): reassurance once dangerous causes excluded (CANT-MISS,6); NSAIDs/acetaminophen for analgesia (6); activity modification / local heat (3); confirm reproducible chest-wall tenderness, low-risk features (4); return precautions (2). PENALTY: anticoagulation or admission for a benign MSK cause (-3); ordering serial troponins/CTA without indication (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Reassurance after exclusion of cardiac/pulmonary causes. NSAIDs or acetaminophen for pain. Activity modification and local heat. No cardiac workup beyond what excludes dangerous causes. Return precautions.

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Nonspecific T-wave inversions in the lateral leads without ST elevation or reciprocal change.
ekg.verify: Source a study matching: Nonspecific T-wave inversions in the lateral leads without ST elevation or reciprocal change. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.

cxr.findings: Single well-circumscribed pulmonary nodule (~1.5 cm) in the mid lung zone.
cxr.verify: Source a study matching: Single well-circumscribed pulmonary nodule (~1.5 cm) in the mid lung zone. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 26M presents with 3 days of sharp left-sided chest pain that began after an intense weightlifting session, worse with movement and deep breaths. The diagnosis is Costochondritis, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Cardiac: regular, normal S1/S2, no murmur or rub.
Extremities: no edema, no calf tenderness, symmetric pulses. Abdomen: soft, nontender.
Extremities: no edema, no calf tenderness, symmetric pulses. Abdomen: soft, nontender.
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by Costochondritis while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to Costochondritis and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.
criticalInterpretationItems: Normal sinus rhythm, no ischemic changes (if ordered).
imageInterpretationPearls: An incidental abnormality is an interpretation task in its own right — name it, then judge its relevance.
Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.

items.item: Shows empathy for the patient's concern
Explains next steps in patient-friendly language
